testing_centers.notes: In-person test centers worldwide and online proctored via OnVUE

id_requirements: Two forms of ID are required. Primary ID must be government-issued, include candidate's name, photo, and signature (e.g. passport, driver's license). Secondary ID must include candidate's name and signature or name and photo.
exam_rules: No notes, scratch paper, electronic devices, books, or study materials permitted. Personal items must be stored. No food or drink except water. Restroom breaks allowed but timer continues. Testing center staff may monitor via video/audio.
renewal_options: Earn CE credits through CompTIA CE program (training, conferences, publishing, teaching, higher certs) or retake the current exam version before expiry.
target_audience: IT support specialist and help desk technician
recommended_experience: 12 months of hands-on experience in an IT support specialist job role

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period required before first retake. After the second failed attempt, candidates must wait 14 calendar days before any subsequent attempt. No limit on total attempts.

objectives.title: Hardware setup
sub_objectives: installing components like batteries, cameras, and Wi-Fi antennas.

objectives.title: Accessory options
sub_objectives: configuring USB, Bluetooth, NFC, and docking stations.

objectives.title: Network setup
sub_objectives: configuring Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, cellular data, and synchronization settings.

objectives.title: Troubleshooting
sub_objectives: identifying and fixing hardware and connectivity issues.

objectives.title: Protocols and ports
sub_objectives: learning about networking protocols, ports, and wireless technologies.

objectives.title: SOHO networks
sub_objectives: setting up small office/home office networks, including IP addressing and VPNs.

objectives.title: Networking tools
sub_objectives: troubleshooting with crimpers, cable testers, and Wi-Fi analyzers.

objectives.title: Component installation
sub_objectives: setting up RAM, CPUs, and storage devices.

objectives.title: Cables and connectors
sub_objectives: working with HDMI, Ethernet, and USB cables.

objectives.title: Peripheral devices
sub_objectives: installing and maintaining printers, scanners, and other peripherals.

objectives.title: Motherboards and power
sub_objectives: configuring motherboards, power supplies, and cooling solutions.

objectives.title: Virtualization concepts
sub_objectives: understanding virtual machines, hypervisors, and desktop virtualization.

objectives.title: Cloud models
sub_objectives: learning about IaaS, SaaS, and PaaS.

objectives.title: Diagnosing issues
sub_objectives: identifying and fixing hardware, network, and connectivity problems.

objectives.title: Troubleshooting tools
sub_objectives: using multimeters, cable testers, and loopback plugs.
